quotes.quote: The system can query the literature database during the idea formulation process to assess the novelty of a proposed concept and identify relevant prior work.
quotes.rationale: In-loop literature tool judging novelty against a prior-art corpus during idea formulation, informing which avenue to pursue.

quotes.quote: After each stage, the experiment manager selects the best-performing node using a dedicated LLM evaluator (see next section) based on clearly articulated criteria. This selected node is then carried forward to seed the subsequent experimentation stage.
quotes.rationale: Dedicated LLM evaluator whose output governs stage transition.

quotes.quote: A best-performing node, selected by LLM-based evaluation, is passed down as the root node of Stage 2.
quotes.rationale: Confirms the selection output seeds the next stage root.

quotes.quote: When choosing between non-buggy nodes, the system uses a best-first search strategy, guided by an LLM that evaluates candidates based on factors like performance metrics, training dynamics, and the quality of generated plots.
quotes.rationale: LLM judgment routed into the expansion policy of the tree search, i.e. search control rather than terminal assessment.

quotes.quote: We incorporate this agentic tree search approach across all four experimentation stages outlined in §3.2.1, enabling deeper and more systematic exploration of scientific hypotheses.
quotes.rationale: Establishes the branching search that this channel controls.

quotes.quote: Any issues flagged by the VLM (such as unclear labels, missing legends, or misleading visualizations) result in the node being marked as buggy, and this feedback is recorded for future debugging.
quotes.rationale: VLM critique changes node status and is retained in the tree for later debugging.

quotes.quote: For buggy nodes, the system attempts to debug them using stored error information.
quotes.rationale: Execution status with stored error information drives child-node generation.

quotes.quote: With a predefined probability, a buggy node is chosen (thus prioritizing error resolution and debugging); otherwise, a non-buggy node is selected for further refinement and improvement.
quotes.rationale: Buggy/non-buggy status is consumed by the expansion policy.

quotes.quote: Blind Peer Review: The three AI-generated submissions were included among the 43 total submissions received by the ICBINB workshop. Reviewers were informed in advance that some submissions might be AI-generated, but were not told which submissions were produced by The AI Scientist-v2.
quotes.rationale: Independent external human reviewers assessing the finished manuscripts under blind review.

quotes.quote: one manuscript achieved a sufficiently high average reviewer score (6.33 out of 10, with individual scores of 6, 6, and 7) to surpass the workshop’s acceptance threshold. The remaining two submissions received lower scores and were not accepted.
quotes.rationale: Acceptance outcome; terminal, with no reported routing back into the system.

quotes.quote: From this combined AI-generated pool, we selected the three most promising initial ideas–two from the first batch, and one from the second batch–based on alignment with the workshop theme and potential interest
quotes.rationale: Author-side human gate deciding which ideas enter the full pipeline.

quotes.quote: humans initiated the process by providing the high-level workshop theme and selected which initial AI-generated ideas to run multiple times through the full pipeline (akin to deciding which experiments to fund or prioritize)
quotes.rationale: Confirms the human gate sits outside the autonomous single-run loop.

quotes.quote: we selected the single best-resulting manuscript for submission based on a careful inspection of its overall coherence and scientific quality
quotes.rationale: Human terminal gate choosing the artifact submitted for external review; no reported return path into the system.

policy_type: agentic tree search over experiment nodes across four experimentation stages
probabilistic buggy-vs-non-buggy node selection with LLM-guided best-first search among non-buggy nodes
stage-wise experiment manager with LLM best-node promotion and checkpointing
template-free experimentation (no fixed human-authored template code)
memory_structure: experiment tree of nodes carrying buggy/non-buggy status
stored error information used for debugging
recorded VLM critique feedback retained for future debugging
stage-completion checkpoints recorded by the experiment manager

diagnostics.statement: No reliability study of any kind is reported for the dedicated LLM node evaluator that selects the node seeding the next experimentation stage.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After each stage, the experiment manager selects the best-performing node using a dedicated LLM evaluator (see next section) based on clearly articulated criteria.
A best-performing node, selected by LLM-based evaluation, is passed down as the root node of Stage 2.

diagnostics.consequence: An unvalidated LLM judgment determines which experimental branch survives into later stages, so systematic evaluator error would propagate silently through the remainder of the run.

diagnostics.statement: The VLM plot critique simultaneously diagnoses presentation issues and gates node status, marking a node buggy for defects such as unclear labels or missing legends.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Any issues flagged by the VLM (such as unclear labels, missing legends, or misleading visualizations) result in the node being marked as buggy, and this feedback is recorded for future debugging.

diagnostics.consequence: Presentation-level critique is conflated with an executability gate, so cosmetically imperfect but scientifically sound nodes can be demoted and deprioritized by the search policy.

diagnostics.statement: The only independent evaluation reported is external workshop peer review, and the packet reports no routing of reviewer feedback back into the experiment tree or later runs.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The three AI-generated submissions were included among the 43 total submissions received by the ICBINB workshop.
The remaining two submissions received lower scores and were not accepted.

diagnostics.consequence: The system's only independent quality signal cannot correct search or memory, leaving all closed-loop computational feedback internal and self-evaluated.

diagnostics.statement: The in-loop novelty check against a literature database is described but no evaluation of its prior-art coverage or verdict quality is reported for that channel.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The system can query the literature database during the idea formulation process to assess the novelty of a proposed concept and identify relevant prior work.

diagnostics.consequence: Novelty claims entering the pipeline rest on an unaudited ideation-stage judgment that is distinct from the terminal human reviewers, so novelty-specific errors are undetectable within the packet.

diagnostics.statement: Human authors act as selection gates both before runs (which ideas to fund) and after runs (which manuscript to submit), while autonomy is claimed only within any single run.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This initial idea selection step allowed us to manage computational resources by choosing which distinct, AI-generated starting points to explore further with the full system.
This process mimics a professor reviewing the work of many students or teams and deciding which work is ready to be submitted for peer review.
the entire process within any single run—hypothesis refinement, code generation, execution, analysis, visualization, and writing—was performed autonomously

diagnostics.consequence: The reported acceptance outcome reflects a human-filtered best-of-many pipeline, so the system boundary must be stated explicitly before autonomy or acceptance rates are compared across systems.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After each stage, the experiment manager selects the best-performing node using a dedicated LLM evaluator (see next section) based on clearly articulated criteria.

success_check.observable: Agreement and systematic-error rate of the LLM node evaluator's stage-promotion choices against held-out measured node performance
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Agreement with the metric-optimal node exceeding a pre-registered baseline such as random or greedy-metric selection, with error direction characterized
success_check.evidence_needed: A per-stage study over logged tree nodes comparing evaluator rankings to recorded performance metrics

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Nodes that successfully execute and pass the VLM review without issue are designated as non-buggy.

success_check.observable: Node status derived from execution and correctness signals, with presentation-quality critique recorded as a separate non-gating annotation
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Zero nodes marked buggy solely on cosmetic plot defects in a logged run
success_check.evidence_needed: Node-status logs recording the triggering signal category for each buggy designation

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Reviewers could also opt out of reviewing potentially AI-generated manuscripts.
one manuscript achieved a sufficiently high average reviewer score (6.33 out of 10, with individual scores of 6, 6, and 7) to surpass the workshop’s acceptance threshold.

success_check.observable: Documented mechanism routing external reviewer critiques into tree memory or a subsequent run
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: At least one reported run in which reviewer-derived feedback demonstrably altered node expansion or manuscript revision
success_check.evidence_needed: Run traces linking specific reviewer comments to concrete downstream node or draft changes

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This allows for more informed decisions about pursuing a particular research avenue, ensuring ideas are grounded in the existing scientific landscape from the outset, rather than relying solely on post-hoc checks.

success_check.observable: Audited novelty verdicts from the ideation literature check on a labelled prior-art sample
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reported false-novel rate on ideas with known prior art below a pre-registered threshold
success_check.evidence_needed: A labelled prior-art benchmark with per-idea verdicts and independent adjudication

exact_quotes.exact_quote: It did not involve modifying the ideas themselves.

humans initiated the process by providing the high-level workshop theme and selected which initial AI-generated ideas to run multiple times through the full pipeline (akin to deciding which experiments to fund or prioritize), and subsequently selected the most promising complete output from those multiple runs

success_check.observable: Explicit reporting of candidate counts at each human filtering step alongside unfiltered outcomes
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Acceptance-relevant quality reported for all generated manuscripts, not only the human-selected subset
success_check.evidence_needed: A full generation and selection ledger with per-idea and per-seed outcomes
